Please refer to the appropriate style manual or other sources if you have any questions. Our editors will review what you’ve submitted and determine whether to revise the article. # Dufourspitze. Encyclopaedia Britannica's editors oversee subject areas in which they have extensive knowledge, whether from years of experience gained by working on that content or via study for an advanced degree.... **Dufourspitze**, highest peak (15,203 feet [4,634 m]) of Switzerland and second highest of the Alps, lying 28 miles (45 km) south-southwest of Brig in the Monte Rosa Massif of the Pennine Alps near the Italian border. The summit of the mountain was first reached by an English party in 1855. The peak was named after General Guillaume-Henri Dufour, the head of the survey that first fixed instrumentally the positions of several Monte Rosa peaks.

title: Monte Rosa - Wikipedia

title: Dufourspitze - Wikipedia
content: Dufourspitze is the highest mountain of both Switzerland and the Pennine Alps and is also the second-highest mountain of the Alps and Western Europe.

title: Climb The Dufourspitze – Monte Rosa 4634m – High Mountain Guides
content: # Climb The Dufourspitze – Monte Rosa 4634m. The Dufourspitze is the highest peak of the Monterosa Massif which forms a stunning high mountain range high on the Swiss Italian alpine border. An ascent of this magnificent peak represents an inspiring mountaineering challenge with both significant physical and technical elements meaning some good training is required in advance as well as pre summit climb acclimatisation. The superb ‘new’ Monte Rosa Hut as Base Camp only further adds to the appeal of this wonderful trip deep in to what is perhaps the most spectacular high mountain skyline of the entire European Alps. It is necessary to do either one of our other mountaineering courses, like the Weissmies, Lagginhorn or Matterhorn training programmes before joining this trip, or, if you are sufficiently confident and have good existing mountaineering skills, you can do your own pre course acclimatisation, with at least one night spent sleeping in a high mountain hut.
